source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
Dominoes are said to be arranged correctly if, for each pair of adjacent dominoes, the numbers of spots on the adjacent ends are equal. Paul laid six dominoes in a line as shown in the diagram.
<image1>
He can make a move either by swapping the position of any two dominoes (without rotating either domino) or by rotating one domino. What is the smallest number of moves he needs to make to arrange all the dominoes correctly?

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says 'as shown in the diagram', so the specific domino values/order come from the image
multi_step: task asks for the smallest number of moves, which implies optimization rather than a single direct lookup
allowed operations include both swapping any two dominoes and rotating one domino, requiring comparison of multiple action sequences
correctness condition must hold for each adjacent pair across six dominoes, so the solution depends on combining constraints over the whole line
verifiability: answer_type is numeric and answer_text='3', giving a clear target format
the move rules and correctness criterion are explicit, so a proposed solution can be checked against the stated constraints
once the domino faces/order are read from the image, the minimum-move answer is in principle checkable by exhaustive or systematic search

risk_flags: minimum-move verification depends on image-grounded state interpretation
requires accurate visual extraction of the domino faces and initial order from the diagram

decision_reasons: 题干、图像和答案都齐全，asset registry passed 且无显式 issues
虽然该题强依赖图像，但图像资源已存在且可用，核心语义支撑完整
答案为明确的数字型结果，规则和可验证性较强，适合进入候选池
